content.text: The ADC driver supports the execution of its APIs in parallel from all CPU cores. The user should allocate resources of the EVADC to the CPU cores at pre-compile time using the Resource Manager module

items: The following are the key points to be considered with respect to multicore in the driver:
ADC hardware groups can be allocated to the CPU cores at pre-compile time. For example, EVADC_G0 and EVADC_G1 can be allocated to core-2, EVADC_G9 to core-3 and EVADC_G3 to core-0
An AdcChannel group belongs to a particular hardware group. Hence, all the AdcChannel groups belonging to a hardware group, get allocated to the same core as the hardware group
It must be ensured that AdcChannel group passed as a parameter while invoking an API belongs to the same core on which the API is invoked
DETs will be raised in case APIs are invoked with mismatch of core and AdcChannel group
Interrupts raised by a hardware group must be serviced by the CPU core to which the hardware group has been allocated to
ADC MCAL from IFX – Multi core capable
Adc Controller – not capable of multi core

content.text: Create an IoHwAb module (IoHwAb_Adc) as a multicore module of Type 2
IoHwAb_Adc Mainfunction cyclically reads ADC value of all channels (Also does the ADC compensation and variant handling) and writes into shared memory
For each ADC channel there will be a respective IoHwAb_Adc_xx API when called by user will return values from shared memory.
